pi_items.source: Executed SQL COUNT(*) on the 'customer' table of the Sakila database

Executed Python script implementing the exact prompt logic (favorite category tie-breaking, unseen constraints, and sorting) on an SQLite clone of the Sakila database; verified every customer has exactly 3 candidates (599 * 3 = 1797)

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the official MySQL Sakila sample database, a purely synthetic dataset representing a fictional DVD rental store. The expected customer count was verified as exactly 599. The requirement directs the agent to generate exactly 3 new film recommendations per customer. By executing a custom Python script mirroring the explicit logic (favorite category with alphabetical tie-breaking, omitting previously rented films, and ordering by rental_rate and title), it was confirmed that every single customer receives 3 valid recommendations. This validates the exact total of 1,797 expected rows. All target database objects and output paths precisely match the instructions in the prompt.
